exact_answer: Cushing Disease is specifically caused by a pituitary tumor secreting excess ACTH, while Cushing Syndrome is the broader condition of hypercortisolism from any cause
Cushing's disease refers to a pituitary source of excess ACTH, whereas Cushing's syndrome encompasses all causes of endogenous hypercortisolism including ectopic ACTH and adrenal tumors
Cushing's disease is the pituitary form accounting for 70% of cases, while Cushing's syndrome includes pituitary, ectopic ACTH, and adrenal causes
